name: Headache
meta_description: Learn about headache types, causes, symptoms, and treatment options. Find out when to seek medical care.
description: A headache is pain or discomfort in the head or upper neck region. It can range from mild to severe and may have various causes.
what_it_feels_like: Headaches can feel like dull pressure, sharp throbbing, or tight squeezing. The pain may be localized to one area or spread across the entire head.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical care for severe, sudden headaches, headaches accompanied by neurological symptoms, or persistent headaches.

name: Fatigue
meta_description: Understanding fatigue: causes, symptoms, and effective strategies for overcoming tiredness.
description: Fatigue is a state of extreme tiredness or exhaustion that doesn't improve with rest. It can affect physical and mental functioning.
what_it_feels_like: Fatigue feels like overwhelming tiredness, lack of energy, difficulty concentrating, and decreased motivation. Activities that were once easy become challenging.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek help if fatigue persists despite adequate rest, or is accompanied by weight loss, pain, or other concerning symptoms.

name: Sore Throat
meta_description: Learn about sore throat causes, symptoms, home remedies, and when to see a doctor.
description: A sore throat is pain, scratchiness, or irritation of the throat that often worsens when swallowing.
what_it_feels_like: A sore throat feels like a scratchy, burning, or raw sensation in the throat. Swallowing may be painful or difficult.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek care if sore throat lasts more than a week, is severe, or accompanied by high fever, difficulty breathing, or swollen glands.

name: Nausea
meta_description: Understanding nausea: causes, symptoms, and effective ways to relieve queasiness.
description: Nausea is a feeling of discomfort in the stomach with an urge to vomit. It can be a symptom of many different conditions.
what_it_feels_like: Nausea feels like a queasy, unsettled feeling in the stomach. It may be accompanied by sweating, dizziness, or an urge to throw up.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek emergency care for persistent vomiting, severe abdominal pain, blood in vomit, or signs of dehydration.

name: Dizziness
meta_description: Learn about dizziness causes, symptoms, and effective management strategies for balance issues.
description: Dizziness is a sensation of lightheadedness, unsteadiness, or feeling like you might faint.
what_it_feels_like: Dizziness feels like spinning, swaying, or feeling unbalanced. It may be accompanied by nausea, blurred vision, or weakness.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek care for severe or persistent dizziness, dizziness after head injury, or dizziness accompanied by chest pain.

name: Fever
meta_description: Understanding fever: causes, symptoms, and when to seek medical attention for elevated temperature.
description: A fever is a temporary increase in body temperature, often caused by an infection or illness.
what_it_feels_like: With a fever, you may feel hot, flushed, or chilled. You may have chills, sweating, headache, or muscle aches.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek care for fever over 104°F (40°C), persistent fever, fever with severe headache, or fever in infants under 3 months.

name: Cough
meta_description: Learn about cough causes, types, home remedies, and when to see a doctor for persistent coughing.
description: A cough is a reflex action to clear the throat and airways of mucus, irritants, or foreign particles.
what_it_feels_like: A cough feels like an irritation in the throat or chest that triggers a sudden expulsion of air. It may be dry or productive (with mucus).
when_to_see_doctor: Seek care for persistent cough (more than 3 weeks), cough with blood, chest pain, or difficulty breathing.

name: Back Pain
meta_description: Understanding back pain: causes, symptoms, and effective treatment options for spinal health.
description: Back pain is discomfort or pain in the back, ranging from mild to severe. It can affect the upper, middle, or lower back.
what_it_feels_like: Back pain feels like dull aching, sharp stabbing, or burning. It may be constant or intermittent, and may radiate to other areas.
when_to_see_doctor: Seek care for severe back pain, pain radiating to legs, numbness or tingling, or loss of bladder control.
